preview_text:
The model favors Belgium to win at 37%, though the margin over Senegal is modest at 6 percentage points. Senegal's chances stand at 31%, leaving the teams closely bunched in a genuinely competitive forecast. A draw appears plausible at nearly 32%, reflecting the statistical likelihood of a stalemate outcome.

The narrow spread across all three outcomes indicates a match with no clear separation. Belgium holds a slight edge in the model's view, but Senegal enters with realistic odds of victory. The draw probability—sitting between the two teams' win chances—suggests the match could unfold in multiple ways without a decisive favorite emerging in either team's favor.

The market consensus departs from the model by 7 percentage points on the Belgium win, assigning them 44% versus the model's 37%. Sportsbooks and Polymarket both lean materially more bullish on Belgium than the statistical forecast, while pricing Senegal approximately 5 percentage points lower. The dashboard makes no claim about which source better reflects the true probabilities; the divergence is notable enough to warrant tracking.
